units.title: Variables and Input
units.prompt: Read two integers from stdin and print their sum.
hints: Convert values to numbers before adding.
units.starterCode:
import sys

parts = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
a = int(parts[0]) if len(parts) > 0 else 0
b = int(parts[1]) if len(parts) > 1 else 0
# TODO: print the real sum
print(0)

units.title: Loops
units.prompt: Read n and print the sum of numbers from 1 to n.

import sys

raw = sys.stdin.read().strip()
n = int(raw) if raw else 0
# TODO: compute 1..n
print(0)

units.title: Functions
units.prompt: Create is_even(x) and print true if x is even, else false.
hints: Return a boolean from your function.
units.starterCode:
import sys

def is_even(x: int) -> bool:
    # TODO
    return False

raw = sys.stdin.read().strip()
value = int(raw) if raw else 0
print('true' if is_even(value) else 'false')

units.title: Basic DSA - Two Sum
units.prompt: Line 1: space-separated ints, line 2: target. Print two indices i j that sum to target.
hints: At each element, look for target - value.
units.starterCode:
import sys

lines = [line.strip() for line in sys.stdin.read().splitlines() if line.strip()]
nums = [int(x) for x in lines[0].split()] if lines else []
target = int(lines[1]) if len(lines) > 1 else 0

# TODO: two sum
print('-1 -1')
